t_ch: 闲暇之余都做些什么呢？
t_en: What do you do in your free time?

t_ch: 自然是以牌会友，切磋牌技了。牌友齐聚后，先喝上一桶冰鲜柠檬青梅气泡茶清醒头脑，再算出一个必赢风水位，这点很重要。落座后，就可以血战到天亮咯。
t_en: Naturally, it's all about bonding over playing cards and sharpening our skills. Once my companions gather, first we down a bucket of iced lemon lazuli soda to clear our heads, then figure out the lucky feng shui seats. That part's crucial. Once we're seated, we can battle it out till dawn.

t_ch: 有这么好玩吗？
t_en: Is it really that fun?

t_ch: 当然。牌桌之上，人人赛神仙，没有感情，玩的就是人情世故。平日里没处使的心眼子，到了牌桌上便是各显神通。
t_en: Of course. At the card table, everyone's a celestial being, and there's no feelings involved. It's all about playing the social game. All those schemes you can't use in daily life? At the card table, everyone shows their true colors.

t_ch: 多思考多观察——摸牌出牌得掐准时机，为自己谋算赢面；眼观六路耳听八方，这是察言观势；控下家、盯上家、防对家，这是统合全局。
t_en: Think hard, watch closely. Timing your draws and plays is key to creating your opportunity for victory. Keep your eyes peeled and ears open to read the situation. Control the player to your right, watch the one on your left, and guard against the one across you. That's how you dominate the game.

t_ch: 当然了，如果能拥有百分之九十九的运气，就再好不过了。
t_en: Of course, if I could have ninety-nine percent luck, that'd be even better.

t_ch: 什么牌！想学！
t_en: What cards? I wanna learn!

t_ch: 牌类泛泛。传统的「帝垣琼玉」、煊赫一时的「掼三家」，新晋的「捉鸡二五八」，「心眼牌」…好巧不巧我均有涉及，连胜更是不在话下。
t_en: Card games in general. The traditional "Celestial Jade," the once-illustrious "Three-Man Guandan Poker," the rising "2-5-8 Tiles," "Mind's Eye"... As luck would have it, I'm well-versed in all of them, and winning streaks are a given.

t_ch: 师出爻老板，保你回回稳操胜券。
t_en: Under my wing, you're guaranteed a win every time.

t_ch: 除了青雀，还有谁是牌迷？
t_en: Besides Qingque, who else is a card game fanatic?

t_ch: 那是多了去了，仙舟谁还不会打牌？就说景元、飞霄、怀炎，别看他们平日里对此不露声色，真要招呼他们打牌时，那牌桌上的心眼也是多得数不清哟。
t_en: Oh, there's tons of them. Who on the Xianzhou doesn't know how to play cards? Take Jing Yuan, Feixiao, and Huaiyan, for example: they might act all calm and collected about it on the surface, but once you get them at the card table, the sheer number of tricks up their sleeves is off the charts.

t_ch: 好在爻老板我精通些见招拆招的本事——对青雀这样的老手，要打得明中藏暗，暗里有明，如此才有赢面。对景元就像剥洋葱，需得张张盘算，引得他把心思摊在台面上；对飞霄，要牌行险着，出奇方可制胜；对怀炎，得不停迷惑他的思路，真亦假来假亦真。
t_en: Luckily, I've got a knack for adapting on the fly. Against a veteran like Qingque, you gotta play it subtle with hidden moves wrapped in plain sight, and obvious plays hiding your real strategy. That's the only way to win. With Jing Yuan, it's like peeling an onion, you have to calculate every card, and lure him into laying all his strategy bare. Against Feixiao, you need risky plays and unexpected moves to secure victory. And with Huaiyan, you gotta keep him guessing, blur the lines between truth and bluff.

t_ch: 最有趣的当属我师妹，她没打过什么牌，某日被我生拉硬拽上了高手局，竟遇强则奇袭，有什么出什么，缺什么要什么，最后险胜一局，破了我的二十连胜。
t_en: The most interesting one is definitely my junior soror. She'd never really played before, but one day I dragged her kicking and screaming into a high-stakes match. Against all odds, she went on a full offense, playing whatever she had and demanding for whatever she needed. In the end, she pulled off a narrow victory that shattered my twenty-game winning streak.

t_ch: 牌桌上的新鲜事，怕是讲上几个日夜也难以讲完呐。
t_en: The interesting stories from the card tables? Man, I could go on for days and still not finish them all.

t_ch: 说得我手又痒了。前不久倒是听闻了二相乐园时兴的「银河战力党」，来都来了，改日我也要浅露一手。
t_en: Now you're making my fingers itch. I heard the Cosmicon Collective's all the rage in Planarcadia these days. Since I'm already here, I'll have to show them what I've got sometime soon.

t_ch: 戎韬将军——胸有甲兵，文韬武略！
t_en: Seer Strategist! Armed with military might and wisdom!

t_ch: 小朋友，打官腔的辞令从哪儿学的？我猜猜…这般大实话，难道是飞霄景元他们教你的？
t_en: Child, where'd you learn to spout such rigid formalities? Let me guess... such blunt honesty... Perhaps it was Feixiao or Jing Yuan who taught you that?

t_ch: 但本座不务空名，要论为仙舟人化险为夷、否极生泰之事，我确实做了不少。
t_en: But I live up to my title. When it comes to turning the tables for Xianzhou and creating opportunities in the worst calamities, I've definitely contributed my fair share.

t_ch: 比如？
t_en: Like what?

t_ch: 有我厉害吗？
t_en: More than even me?

t_ch: 譬如精准测算公司旗下各行业股价的涨落行情，狠狠从公司身上薅回来一把被他们算计走的军需资产。虽说太卜司有「三不占」的规矩，但此回例外，也算是以眼还眼咯。
t_en: For example, precisely calculating the stock price fluctuations across the IPC's various industries, and fiercely clawing back a chunk of the military assets they swindled from us. Although the Divination Commission has its "three taboos," this was an exception. You can say it was an eye for an eye.

t_ch: 又譬如点拨了两位有大凶之兆之人，其中一位排除万难，终是辞了医士入了云骑，另一位退休司书知难而进，圆梦当了匠作——这命路啊，就此逢凶化吉，晦而转明。
t_en: And, I advised two individuals plagued by omens of Great Woe. One overcame countless obstacles and eventually left their position as a healer to join the Cloud Knights, while the other, a retired scribe, pressed forward despite the difficulties and fulfilled their dream of becoming a craftsman. Thus, their fates turned Woe into Weal, darkness into light.

t_ch: 真好，只愿人人都能稳步行于自己的康庄大道上，向着未来前进。
t_en: How wonderful. May everyone walk steadily on their own path to glory, marching forward into the future.

t_ch: 最近遇到的新鲜事……
t_en: Anything new going on lately?

t_ch: 新鲜事啊，待我看看……
t_en: Oh, something new? Let me see...

t_ch: 噢，泊地站台旁的寄存柜最右边上面一格里，有只被我解救过后又自己待进去的尘灵；二相乐园的最酸小酒馆仍在闭店整改，不知他们的「超酸无比酸酸挑战」什么时候才能报名……
t_en: Oh, there's a wispae in the top-right locker by the Perdido Platform, I rescued it once, but it just crawled right back in. The Souriest Tavern in Planarcadia is still closed for renovations, I wonder when they'll reopen the registration for their "Ultra-Mega-Super-Sour Challenge"...

t_ch: 嗯…也唯有那重因凶命集结而凝聚成的红色，说是比先前更甚也不为过。
t_en: Hmm... That crimson hue condensed by the convergence of misfortune and woes. It's definitely intensified compared to before.

t_ch: 凶命集结？！
t_en: Woes converging!?

t_ch: 卜筮之道有云：「易穷则变，变则通，通则久」。即便是死局，将自身命数化作变量，也能破得一线生机。
t_en: The way of the divination says: "When things are at exhaustion, changes pave a path. Through that path, they endure." Even at an impasse, by turning your own destiny into a variable, an opportunity for survival will appear.

t_ch: 现在还能看见？
t_en: You can still see it now?

t_ch: 不错。接掌将军符节后，我这双眼睛便可观众生万象，判读气运。
t_en: Indeed. After taking up the General's talisman, these eyes of mine can observe the myriad forms of all beings and divine the flow of fortune.

t_ch: 这处「欢愉」土地上的命兆暗流无时无刻不在提醒我，一场以生命为界限的博弈，才将将开始。
t_en: The undercurrents of fate in this land of "Elation" constantly remind me that a game with life itself on the line is only just beginning.

t_ch: 哼。卜筮之道有云：「易穷则变，变则通，通则久」。即便是死局，将自身命数化作变量，也能破得一线生机。
t_en: Hmph. The way of the divination says: "When things are at exhaustion, changes pave a path. Through that path, they endure." Even at an impasse, by turning your own destiny into a variable, an opportunity for survival will appear.

t_ch: 好了，这一趟公费造访二相乐园，可真是不白来。待回到仙舟，我可要在「孩子们最崇拜的将军」榜上，狠狠赢上一筹。
t_en: Well, this business-sponsored trip to Planarcadia was definitely worth it. When I get back to the Xianzhou, I'll crush it on the "General Most Admired by Kids" rankings.

t_ch: 我问完了，谢谢爻老板。
t_en: I don't have any more questions. Thanks, Madam Yao.

t_ch: 希望你的疑惑都得到了解答。
t_en: I hope you've clarified your doubts.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了，谢谢爻老板。
t_en: That's all I needed to know. Thanks, Madam Yao.

t_ch: 不客气，下次再聊噢。
t_en: No problem! Catch you later!

t_ch: 瞧你，喝一点儿就哇哇吐…想在大城市混，笑点这么低可不行！还得练！
t_en: Look at you, puking your guts up after just a sip or two... Wanna make it in the big city? Then don't have such a weak tolerance! Toughen up!

t_ch: 来城里找工作这么多天，除了老哥你，我就没遇着一个好人！这样，来个笑点最高的，这瓶小弟请！
t_en: I've been lookin' for work in town for days now, and you're the only decent person I've met! Tell you what, let's have the strongest one they've got, and this one's on me!

t_ch: （听听他们在聊什么）
t_en: (Listen to what they are saying)

t_ch: 你这样的我见多了，说真的，别焦虑，工作总会有的。
t_en: I've seen plenty of people like you. Honestly, don't stress about it. You'll find work eventually.

t_ch: 哎！我的母星可没有二相乐园这么复杂，来了二维市才知道什么叫花花世界迷人眼——那高楼、那广场、那火花带闪电的雷虬…我真想留下！
t_en: Hey! My home planet wasn't nearly as complex as Planarcadia. I had no idea what "dazzlin'" meant before I came to Duomension City! Those neon lights, that Plaza, those lightning-sparking thundercoil... I don't want to go nowhere!

t_ch: 可是大哥，我投了<unbreak>1000</unbreak>份简历，只有9个进面，奋斗至今…还是0个offer！世人都笑我，偏偏我还最好笑……
t_en: But friend, I sent out <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> resumes, only got 9 interviews, and after all that hustle... ZERO offers! Everyone's laughin' at me, I'm the biggest joke of 'em all...

t_ch: 欸，别这样贬低自己。你也算是…要力气有力气！要学历…有力气，要背景、要背景也有力气！
t_en: Hey, don't sell yourself short like that. I mean, you've got... strength out the wazoo! And an education... well, you've got strength! And connections? You've definitely got strength!

t_ch: 光有力气啊！呜呜呜……
t_en: I'm nothin' but a musclehead! Waaah...

t_ch: （继续听）
t_en: (Keep listening.)

t_ch: 你居然哭了！哦哟，乐子神在上，无意冒犯，您可千万看清楚，别连累我…@小帅（奋斗版），你到底懂不懂规矩！
t_en: You're actually crying! Hoo boy, by The Laughter above, no offense, but you need to get something straight or you'll get me in trouble... @Chad (Locking In), don't you know the rules at all?

t_ch: 什、什么规矩？
t_en: Wh—What rules?

t_ch: 「世界尽头」只能哈哈大笑！在这里伤心落泪的人会被诅咒——诅咒这辈子都找不到工作！
t_en: At the World's End, you can only laugh out loud! Anyone who cries here will be cursed! Cursed to never find a job in their whole life!

t_ch: 啊！乐子神在上，我现在笑，哈哈哈哈，千万别诅咒我呜呜……
t_en: Aaaaah! By The Laughter, I'm laughing now, hahaha, please, please don't curse me, waaah...

t_ch: 别哭了！还不快去摸摸马桶，高喊「马咪马咪——桶！」，破除诅咒！
t_en: Stop crying! Quick, you need to break the curse! Run to the bathroom, touch the toilet, and shout "Loo-loo, flush it like poo!" at the top of your lungs!

t_ch: …好、好！我听你的！马桶在哪？
t_en: ...O—Okay! Right away! Where's the toilet?

t_ch: 瞧瞧，他信了哈哈哈哈，他居然真的信了，哈哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Would you look at that, he fell for it! HAHAHA, I can't believe he actually bought it, HAHAHAHAHA!

t_ch: （不听了）
t_en: (Stop listening)

t_ch: （不听了）
t_en: (Stop listening)

t_ch: 马桶潺潺湿我衫，文思比它还要干！
t_en: The toilet's splashing water on my shirt, but my thoughts are drier than it!

t_ch: 想不出诗句？
t_en: Can't come up with a verse?

t_ch: 灵感升空又坠地，像*粗口*的跳楼机。
t_en: Inspiration rises high, only to fall and hide, like a *profanity* drop tower ride.

t_ch: 就算牢骚无处发…
t_en: Even if there's nowhere to vent your complaints...

t_ch: …你也不能说脏话。
t_en: ...You shouldn't swear.

t_ch: 脏话咽下更心烦，做个好人真困难。文明二字挂嘴边，马桶依旧响潺潺。
t_en: Not swearing just makes me more upset. Being a good person's so hard, you get? Talk of civility's all for show, while the toilet keeps on gurgling below.

t_ch: 还挺押韵！
t_en: That rhymes!

t_ch: 凡夫写诗皆将就，平仄押韵不讲究…呃……
t_en: Amateur poets just wing it, no rhythm or rhyme to bring it... uh...

t_ch: …对牛弹琴令我忧…呃…
t_en: Like talking to a dumb rabbit... Uh...

t_ch: …呃，呃…哞哞哞哞哞哞哞。
t_en: Uh, uh... purr purr meep mit mit!

t_ch: 押、押不上韵了吗？！
t_en: You ran out of rhymes!?

t_ch: 押不上就不要勉强了。
t_en: If you can't find a rhyme, don't try and force one.

t_ch: 此中押韵有真味，佶屈聱牙是大罪！
t_en: Truest rhyme is what I play!\nMangle your words and you shall pay!

t_ch: 你这诗也太水了…
t_en: This poem is so... underwhelming...

t_ch: 我心不动若空堂，赞毁无关我痛痒。
t_en: My heart's still like an empty hall. Neither praise or jibe makes me fall.

t_ch: 黑子请乖乖候场，别染指我的奖赏，否则我要让你投降！哟！我要用，诗歌把心扉叩响。我要让，黄金马桶的光辉，做我奖杯，耀武扬威。哟！
t_en: Haters, sit still and wait your turn, keep your hands off my prize or you'll get burned. Surrender, yo! I'll knock on your heart's door with my poetic flow. My trophy shall be the glory of the golden porcelain throne so watch me shine, my show aglow! Yo!

t_ch: 好家伙，成RAP了！
t_en: Whoa, that's straight-up rap right there!

t_ch: 这还是诗歌吗？
t_en: Is this even poetry anymore?

t_ch: 诗歌要千秋长留，岂能够故步自囚？
t_en: If poetry is to endure with persistence, how can it confine itself into obsolescence?

t_ch: 最好离我远点。
t_en: Please, just stay away from me.

t_ch: 双目垂泪心成灰，有缘雅座再相会！
t_en: Tears stream down my ashen face. We'll meet again by fate's grace!

t_ch: 「…亮明汪身，表明来意…」
t_en: "...Reveal your true identity and intentions..."

t_ch: …不，这感觉不对……
t_en: ...No, that feels off somehow...

t_ch: 你在干什么？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}好，如您所见，我正在为「白兔剧团」的新剧《绒绒大舞台•浣熊传》排练。
t_en: Hello {F#miss}{M#sir}, as you can see, I'm rehearsing for Fluffy Theater: The Raccoon's Tale, a new play by the White Rabbit Troupe.

t_ch: 这部剧改编自闻名二相乐园的《苍天航路绒绒号》，对我，对整个剧团来说都意义重大，所以我希望您能暂时保持安静。谢谢您的配合。
t_en: This play is adapted from the renowned Fluffy Across the Blue from Planarcadia, and so it holds great significance for me and the entire troupe. Therefore, I must kindly ask you to remain quiet for now. Thank you for your cooperation.

t_ch: 莫非你演三月…兔？
t_en: Don't tell me you're playing March... Bunny?

t_ch: 不，三月兔女士由当红女星萨克蒂•洁•蔓露施坦因扮演。而我，将出演那位男人中最优雅的存在，女人眼中最温润的奶油小生……
t_en: No, Ms. March Bunny will be played by the popular actress Shakti J. Mandelstein. And I will play that most elegant of men, the sweetest heartthrob in every woman's dreams...
Mr. Yang!

t_ch: 居然是杨叔！
t_en: Mr. Yang! Really!?

t_ch: 果然是杨叔！
t_en: Mr. Yang! I knew it!

t_ch: 真欣慰，我们的品味如此一致！
t_en: It's so gratifying to know we have the same great taste!

t_ch: 他的双眸常含忧郁的泪水，他的举止令万千少女魂牵梦萦，他蓬松的大尾巴更是犬中极品！我爱他…不、我势必要成为他！
t_en: His eyes, forever brimming with sorrow. His grace, the stuff of a million girls' dreams. And that fluffy tail, absolute pinnacle of all canines! I love him... No, I must become him!

t_ch: 这段剧情超燃的，获得了千万级的点击量！
t_en: This scene is absolutely epic, it got millions of views!

t_ch: 如果能饰演好杨叔这样一位极具魅力的男性角色，或许我也能离成为A组卡司更进一步！
t_en: If I can nail a charismatic male character like Mr. Yang, maybe I'll be one step closer to making it into the A-list cast!

t_ch: 等你有空的时候，就来「白兔剧团」看看吧！虽然《绒绒号》的故事早已脍炙人口，但我们的演绎一定会让你耳目一新！
t_en: Come check out the White Rabbit Troupe as soon as you're free! While The Fluffy story might be a classic, our interpretation will definitely give you a fresh experience!

t_ch: （还是不打扰了）
t_en: (Better not disturb them...)

t_ch: 所以我正在排演他超经典的对峙场景……
t_en: So I'm currently rehearsing his most classic stand-off scene...

t_ch: 「亮明汪身，表明来意，否则，我得请你做好被黑洞撕裂的准备汪！」
t_en: "Reveal your true identity and intentions... Otherwise, brace yourself for disintegration by a black hole!"

t_ch: 这一口下肚，快哉快哉，喝得小生我是飘飘见了星神！
t_en: One gulp of this divine brew, oh what bliss! This humble one is floating so high, I'm seeing Aeons!

t_ch: 噫嘘唏，小生醉眼，举杯对望，竟品出你眸中有三分寂寥，七分疏狂…小生明白，是整个寰宇负了你{RUBY_B#讴}区{RUBY_E#}歌四方的理想啊！
t_en: Ah, my friend... through tipsy eyes I raise my glass, and what do I see? Three portions of loneliness, seven portions of wild abandon in your gaze... I understand now: The entire cosmos has failed your dream of {RUBY_B#sharing}sparing{RUBY_E#} Singafar's ideals across the stars!

t_ch: 我本应在江湖悠悠，做一代文豪——
t_en: I was meant to roam the world freely, becoming a literary legend...

t_ch: 停停停，怎么唱起来了？
t_en: Whoa whoa whoa, why are you breaking into song?

t_ch: 好险好险…小生唐突，再多一句，就要被人{RUBY_B#要版权费}追究窃曲之过{RUBY_E#}了。多谢{F#姑娘}{M#公子}仗义相助！
t_en: Phew, that was a close call... I got a bit carried away. One more line and {RUBY_B#I'd be slapped with a copyright lawsuit}I would've been charged with the crime of song-theft{RUBY_E#}. Many thanks for coming to my rescue, {F#miss}{M#mister}!

t_ch: 小生歌四方，写作数十载，如今仍旧囊中羞涩，皆是怀才不遇、小说无力发表之故。
t_en: I'm Singafar, been writing for decades now, and I'm still flat broke. All because nobody recognizes my genius and I can't get my novels published.

t_ch: 哎，每每思及此处，小生便如{RUBY_B#鲠}鱼{RUBY_E#}在喉，恨不得一命呜呼！苦哉，何故幻月不独照我，何故无人扶我著书之志！
t_en: Ah, whenever I think about this, I feel like there's a {RUBY_B#fishbone}fish{RUBY_E#} stuck in my throat, and I'd rather just die! How cruel! Why doesn't the Phantasmoon shine on me alone? Why is there no one to support my ambition to write books!

t_ch: 想来末法之世，娱乐动画人人沉溺，如吾等颇具文学造{RUBY_B#诣}纸{RUBY_E#}之士已世间罕见。
t_en: In this Mappou Age of the Dharma, everyone's drowning in mindless entertainment and cartoons. Cultured talents who create {RUBY_B#literary}literally{RUBY_E#} masterpieces are practically extinct.

t_ch: 扪心自问，尔等谁能如吾这般常卧榻上，夜不敢寐{RUBY_B#誊}眷{RUBY_E#}抄网络经典，足不出户钻研传统言语之精妙？读不懂小生之作，也是自然。
t_en: Ask yourselves honestly, who among you can claim to spend their days lounging in bed, burning the midnight oil {RUBY_B#copying}transcribing{RUBY_E#} internet classics, and studying the refined nuances of traditional language without ever leaving the house? It's only natural that you can't grasp the brilliance of my work.

t_ch: 然，终有一日，小生定能作出一部寰宇闻名的巨著！
t_en: But one day, I shall create a masterpiece renowned across the universe!

t_ch: 你当务之急是先断网。
t_en: Your top priority is to disconnect from the internet first.

t_ch: 使不得使不得！小生足不出户，却学贯寰宇，皆仰赖于此！
t_en: No, no, I mustn't! I never leave my room, yet I've learned everything under the stars, all thanks to this!

t_ch: 你当务之急是先买本字典。
t_en: Your top priority is getting yourself a dictionary, pal.

t_ch: 妙哉妙哉……
t_en: Marvelous, simply marvelous...

t_ch: ——你说谁文盲呢？
t_en: Are you calling me illiterate?

t_ch: 最后不成功怎么办？
t_en: What if it doesn't work out in the end?

t_ch: 那便来此再饮一杯，又有何妨？
t_en: So what's stopping us from having another drink here?

t_ch: 或许，此即纯粹文艺所需承受之命运乎？呜呼！哀哉！
t_en: Perchance, such is the destiny pure artistry must bear? Alas! How tragic!

t_ch: <size=28>文学已死！艺术尽灭！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Literature is dead! Art is extinct!</size>

t_ch: 听不懂，先走了。
t_en: I don't get it. I'm outta here.

t_ch: 末法之世，文学已死，不理解小生也是自然。
t_en: In this Mappou Age of the Dharma, literature is dead. No wonder you don't get me.

t_ch: 小生…就此别过……
t_en: I... bid you farewell...

t_ch: 欢迎光临克拉肯酒馆！这位客人，里边请——
t_en: Welcome to Kraken's Tavern! Right this way, please.

t_ch: 等等，你的装束看着很熟悉，似乎在哪见过…是啊，我怎么会忘了呢？
t_en: Wait a minute... that outfit is so familiar. I'm sure I've seen it somewhere... Ah, yes. How could I possibly forget?

t_ch: 你还记得什么？
t_en: What else do you remember?

t_ch: 说说看，你觉得我是谁？
t_en: Why don't you tell me who you think I am?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，贵客说笑了。这二相乐园，谁不知道球棒浣熊的名号？反倒是我这个迎客的，没能一眼认出您的模样，怪我，怪我啊！
t_en: Hahaha, you flatter me, my dear guest. Is there anyone in Planarcadia who doesn't know of the great Baseball Raccoon? I'm the one at fault for not recognizing you at a glance. My mistake, truly!

t_ch: 尝尝我亲自捕捞的海鲜吧，这可是二维市的明星餐品，霸占必吃榜数十年，保证美味！
t_en: Have a taste of the seafood I caught myself. It's the star dish of Duomension City, and it's been topping the must-try lists for decades. I guarantee you it's delicious!

t_ch: 你不是个大海妖嘛？
t_en: But aren't you a great sea monster yourself?

t_ch: 怪物开的店，我不敢进。
t_en: I'm not so sure about entering a shop run by a monster.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，看到这些威武的触手了么？它们本为掀起波澜而生，在烹饪方面也不遑多让。
t_en: Hahaha, do you see these magnificent tentacles? They were born to summon the tides, and they're second to none when it comes to food.

t_ch: 但说来惭愧，其实，我不知道该如何兴风作浪。每次前往海边，我心中油然而生的只有一个念头——那便是为顾客带回最优质的食材。
t_en: But it's actually a bit embarrassing that I don't actually know how to command the tides. Every time I go to the sea, only one thought comes to my mind... and that is to bring back the absolute best ingredients for my customers.

t_ch: 或许，我根本就没有被称为海妖的资格吧？不过没关系，我会钻研烹饪之道，努力成为二维市最受欢迎的店长！
t_en: Maybe I don't deserve to be called a sea monster at all, eh? But that's okay. I'll dedicate myself to the art of cooking and strive to become the most popular shopkeeper in Duomension City!

t_ch: 怪物转型当店主，也挺好。
t_en: Nothing wrong with a monster starting a new career as a shopkeeper.

t_ch: 不用感到抱歉。
t_en: You don't have to feel bad about it.

t_ch: 有时候，客人们也会谈起我的过去，猜测我曾是叱咤星海的的大海妖。传言为小酒馆带来了人气，而我正好藉此机会，征服食客的味蕾。
t_en: Sometimes, my guests will talk about my past, speculating that I was once the great and fearsome sea monster who ruled the cosmic seas. The rumors brought in more business, and I took the opportunity to win them over with my cooking.

t_ch: 说到这个，哈哈…要是贵客感兴趣的话，不如来一份招牌海鲜套餐吧？
t_en: Speaking of which, haha... if you're interested, my dear guest, why not try our signature seafood set?

t_ch: 克拉肯饱含工作热情地接待着你，只是这亲切的话语中，再没有往日的桀骜气息。
t_en: Kraken welcomes you with all the passion they have for their work, but their cordial words no longer hold any trace of their former unruly arrogance.

t_ch: 你望向那对眼睛，它们曾承载某位海妖对大海的恐惧与向往，而今透出的，唯有一位店主展露厨艺时的自信与期待。
t_en: You gaze into that pair of eyes. They once carried a certain sea monster's fear of and longing for the ocean, but now all that shines through is the confidence and excitement of a shopkeeper showing off their culinary art.

t_ch: 就来份招牌套餐吧。
t_en: I'll have the signature combo!

t_ch: 好嘞，请您稍等。赌上我的店主生涯，保证能取悦您的舌头！
t_en: Coming right up! And I'll bet my entire career as a shopkeeper that it will be a delight for your taste buds!

t_ch: 欢迎光临克拉肯酒馆，每日海鲜不限量供应，现捞现卖，保证合您口味！
t_en: Welcome to Kraken's Tavern! We offer unlimited fresh seafood daily, caught and served on demand. I guarantee it'll be to your liking!

t_ch: 噢！是大名鼎鼎的球棒浣熊，贵客啊！今天想尝点什么？
t_en: Whoa! It's the one and only Baseball Raccoon! What an honor! What are you in the mood for today?

t_ch: 想和你聊聊…
t_en: I want to chat with you...

t_ch: 没问题。本店长也算见多识广，常从顾客口中听来二相乐园各地的消息。贵客想聊些什么，我都奉陪！
t_en: No problem at all. I've heard a lot in my time, especially the news from all over Planarcadia that my customers bring in. Whatever you want to talk about, I'm happy to oblige!

t_ch: 你是怎么当上店长的？
t_en: So, how did you become the shop's owner?

t_ch: 说实话，我不太记得自己在经营小酒馆之前的事了，印象里，那是一段穷困潦倒的艰难岁月。
t_en: Honestly, I can't recall much from before I started running this tavern. All I remember is a vague impression of a difficult life, full of hardship and poverty.

t_ch: 从外形上看，我是只张牙舞爪的大海妖。可若是如此，又怎会缺乏愿力呢？随便在海上卷起风暴，就会万众瞩目吧，哈哈哈。
t_en: I may look like a ferocious sea monster, but if that were true, how could I have been so short on Wishpower? I should've been able to whip up a storm and grab everyone's attention easily, hahaha.

t_ch: 但我只记得自己饥寒交迫，晕倒在鸽川边，濒临消散。
t_en: All I can remember is being starved and freezing, collapsing by the Dovebrook, soon to fade from existence.

t_ch: 所幸一位路过的噗噜救起了我，并带到小酒馆，询问我是否愿意为它工作。
t_en: Luckily, a passing ploo rescued me and brought me to this tavern, asking if I wanted to work here.

t_ch: 它就是当时的店长，而小酒馆的名字，就叫「克拉肯」。
t_en: The ploo was the shop's owner at the time, and the name of the tavern was, in fact, "Kraken."

t_ch: 从零开始的打工生活。
t_en: A new life, starting from the bottom.

t_ch: 没错。为了纪念此次新生，我便以店名作为自己的新名字。
t_en: Exactly. To commemorate this new beginning, I took the shop's name as my new name.

t_ch: 说来倒也巧，自从我到店里后，慕名而来的顾客越来越多。有海妖在此工作的「克拉肯」小酒馆，也算是名副其实吧。
t_en: As it happens, more and more customers were drawn to the shop after I started working here. I suppose a tavern named "Kraken" with a sea monster on staff really lives up to its name.

t_ch: 这个店名真的会有生意吗…
t_en: I'm surprised you get any business with a name like that...

t_ch: 说来倒也巧，自从我到店里后，慕名而来的顾客越来越多。有海妖在此工作的「克拉肯」小酒馆，算是名副其实吧！
t_en: As it happens, more and more customers were drawn to the shop after I started working here. I suppose a tavern named "Kraken" with a sea monster on staff really lives up to its name!

t_ch: 也因此，为了纪念新生，我便以店名作为自己的新名字了。
t_en: That's also why, to commemorate this new life, I took the shop's name as my new name.

t_ch: 但在生意日渐红火时，那位噗噜却不辞而别，只留下一封信。它说自己厌倦了开店生活，要去追求成为喜剧明星的梦想。
t_en: However, right when business was really picking up, the ploo left without any goodbyes, leaving just a letter behind. It said it was tired of running the tavern and was leaving to pursue its dream of becoming a comedy star.

t_ch: 于是我接过经营重担，成为顾客们熟悉的店主，直至现在。
t_en: So I took over the responsibilities of running the place and became the shopkeeper everyone's familiar with now.

t_ch: 小酒馆的生活忙碌而充实，我将全部心血都灌注在这份事业中。很难说究竟它是我的一部分，或者…我才是它的部分。
t_en: The tavern life is busy but fulfilling. I've poured my entire being into this work. It's hard to say if it's a part of me, or... if I'm just a part of it.

t_ch: 无论如何，将「克拉肯」打造成二相乐园最受欢迎的小酒馆，是我的梦想。
t_en: No matter what, it's my dream to make "Kraken" the most beloved tavern in Planarcadia.

t_ch: 经营小酒馆的秘诀是？
t_en: What's the secret to running a tavern?

t_ch: 莫非，您想要加盟我的店铺？荣幸之至！
t_en: Are you thinking of joining my business as a franchisee? What an honor!

t_ch: 但实不相瞒，这经营秘诀说来简单，却也极难复制。否则我也不会每日驻守店铺、事事亲为了。
t_en: But I'll be honest, the secret is simple to explain but extremely hard to execute. If it weren't, I wouldn't be here at the shop every single day, handling everything myself.

t_ch: 总结起来，就是两句话：使用最好的食材，做出最棒的餐品。
t_en: It all comes down to two things: use the best ingredients, and make the best possible dishes.

t_ch: 细说「食材获取」。
t_en: Tell me more about the best ingredients.

t_ch: 一道鱼生的准备时间，不是从它被摆到砧板上开始计算的，而是从鱼儿诞生在大海里开始计算的。
t_en: The preparation for raw fish slices doesn't start when the fish hits the cutting board. It starts from the moment that fish is born in the sea.

t_ch: 烹饪方式越简单，就越需要优秀的食材。这即是厨艺的真谛——并非改造，而是呈现。
t_en: The simpler the cooking method, the more you need outstanding ingredients. That's the real essence of cooking: not the transformation of something, but presenting it at its best.

t_ch: 由于海妖的特性，我对水产质量极为挑剔。曾有段时间，小酒馆面临着海鲜短缺的窘境。订购生鱼难免磨损，并在储运过程中失去鲜度。
t_en: Thanks to my sea monster nature, I'm extremely picky about seafood quality. The tavern faced a shortage for a while, because ordering raw fish meant it would inevitably get damaged and lose its freshness in transit.

t_ch: 为此，我不得不每日深潜海底，亲自捕捞最上等的食材——没有什么诀窍，靠得便是满腔热情，与对厨艺虔诚的心。
t_en: So, I had to start diving deep into the ocean every day to catch the absolute best ingredients myself. There's no special trick to it, just a whole lot of passion and a reverent heart for cooking.

t_ch: 细说「餐品制作」。
t_en: Tell me more about meal prep.

t_ch: 在最初，我只能笨拙地摹仿店长处理鲜鱼的动作，就像从事某种重复性的体力劳动。
t_en: Well, at first, I could only clumsily copy the owner's fish-processing technique, like I was doing some kind of repetitive manual labor.

t_ch: 不知是否宣传的缘故，「海妖套餐」大受欢迎。在认可与喜爱的滋养下，我的烹饪技艺突飞猛进，最后甚至获得了「神之手」的美誉。
t_en: I'm not sure if it was just good marketing, but the "Sea Monster Combo" became a huge hit. With all that praise and adoration, my cooking skills improved dramatically, and eventually I even earned the honored moniker of Hands of God.

t_ch: 可你根本没有手…
t_en: But you don't have any... hands.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这只是个比喻。我有的是手段来处理食材。
t_en: Hahaha, that's just an expression. I have plenty of ways to handle ingredients, don't you worry.

t_ch: 厨神大海妖！
t_en: A master chef of sea monster!

t_ch: 愿力，很奇妙吧？只要汇聚得足够多，谁都能成就一番事业。
t_en: Wishpower is a curious thing, isn't it? If you can gather enough of it, anyone can make something of themselves.

t_ch: 当我完成烹饪，看到餐品迸发出光芒时，我便知道，它达到了食材能呈现出的最佳状态。可以说，在端上餐桌前，我已先一步品尝到了幸福与满足的滋味。
t_en: When I finish a dish and see it radiate light, I know it has reached the peak form its ingredients could possibly achieve. You could say I get the first taste of happiness and satisfaction, long before it's served.

t_ch: 原来如此，我明白了。
t_en: Oh, I get it now.

t_ch: 嗯，希望我的分享对您有帮助。
t_en: Good. I'm glad if my story was helpful to you.

t_ch: 无论如何，心怀热情与敬意去做喜爱的事，就绝不会出错。
t_en: At the end of the day, you can't go wrong if you do what you love with passion and respect.

t_ch: 还想去看看天外的海吗？
t_en: Do you still want to see the ocean beyond the sky?

t_ch: 您是说…天外？我很好奇，但对我来说，这个梦想实在遥不可及。这副身躯若是离开幻月映照的范围，恐怕支撑不了多久便会消散。
t_en: Beyond... the sky? I do, but... a dream like that is completely out of reach for me. This body would probably dissipate soon after leaving the area illuminated by the Phantasmoon.

t_ch: 我可以只带上你的眼睛。
t_en: I can just bring your eyes with me.

t_ch: 感谢。不过，若真有那天，希望您带上的不是我的眼睛——
t_en: Thank you. But if that day ever comes, I hope it is not my eyes you take with you...

t_ch: 而是我的肚子。
t_en: ...but my stomach instead.

t_ch: 肚子？
t_en: Your stomach?

t_ch: 你也是个大胃袋。
t_en: So you have a huge appetite.

t_ch: 难得出去一趟，只是逛逛未免太过可惜。何况我也尝不出味道来，哈哈哈。
t_en: It would be a waste to go on such a rare trip and only look around. Besides, I can't taste flavors that way, hahaha.

t_ch: 如果可以，我想把其他星球的海装进肚子里，把全星系的优质海鲜带回二相乐园。这样，二维市的顾客足不出户，便可以品尝到来自银河各地的美味食材。
t_en: If it were possible, I'd want to hold the oceans of other worlds in my belly and bring all the finest seafood in the galaxies back to Planarcadia. This way, the people of Duomension City could taste delicious ingredients from across the cosmos without ever leaving town.

t_ch: 不过即便如此，因为要容纳银河的缘故，需要的愿力也是个天文数字吧。
t_en: Still, the Wishpower required to contain the entire cosmos would be, well, astronomical.

t_ch: 所以，就当是个美丽的梦——离开二相乐园，前往天外的世界，对我们幻造种而言还是太过遥远了。
t_en: So, let's just call it a dream and leave it at that. For us imagenae, leaving Planarcadia for the world beyond the sky is still something far beyond our reach.

t_ch: 没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all I wanted to ask.

t_ch: 嗯。贵客有需要的时候，随时来找本店长就好。
t_en: Very well. If you ever need anything, don't hesitate to come find me.

t_ch: 还是来份招牌套餐吧！
t_en: I think I'll have the signature combo!

t_ch: 好嘞，请稍等。招牌套餐，马上就来！
t_en: You got it! Just a moment, please. One signature combo, coming right up!

t_ch: 我还有事情要处理。
t_en: I have other matters to take care of.

t_ch: 嗯。贵客有需要的时候，随时来找本店长就好。
t_en: Very well. If you ever need anything, don't hesitate to come find me.

t_ch: 嗯。贵客有需要的时候，随时来找本店长就好。
t_en: Very well. If you ever need anything, don't hesitate to come find me.

t_ch: 可疑的电话亭，阵阵清脆的铃声。当你满怀好奇提起听筒时——没有问候、没有抱歉、没有控诉、也没有推销——只有强劲的电流声瞬间击穿你的脑海。你的耳膜嗡嗡作响。
t_en: A suspicious phone booth, ringing with crisp, clear chimes. When you curiously pick up the receiver, there is no greeting, no apology, no accusation, no sales pitch. Just a surge of electrical current that instantly pierces through your mind. Your eardrums buzz and ring.

t_ch: 难道是电话坏了？
t_en: Is the phone busted?

t_ch: 难道是恶作剧？
t_en: Is this some kind of prank?

t_ch: 难道是无差别袭击？
t_en: Is this an indiscriminate attack?

t_ch: 难道是都市怪谈？
t_en: Could this be an urban myth?

t_ch: 难道是你？
t_en: Could it be you?

t_ch: 你说不准。在二相乐园，只有你想不到的，没有你见不到的。
t_en: You never know. In Planarcadia, there's nothing that's impossible to see.

t_ch: 事实上，这恰恰是愿力的作用原理：你甚至可以想象一块超现实的面板，调节环境音量，以防遭到历史上某位幻月游戏参赛者亡魂的袭击……
t_en: In fact, that's exactly how Wishpower works: you could even imagine a surreal control panel, adjusting the ambient volume to protect yourself from attacks by the lingering spirits of past Phantasmoon Games contestants...

t_ch: …嘿，等等，听！那毫无规律的杂讯里，似乎存在着某种逻辑——某种声音——
t_en: ...Hey, wait, listen! Within that chaotic noise, there seems to be some kind of logic, some kind of voice...

t_ch: 一连串陌生的名字在浑浊的电流中浮现。电话那头的人或某物确实存在。他念出了他们。她在寻找他们。它的声音压抑、冰冷，仿佛正拖拽着生锈的病床在太平间游荡。
t_en: A string of unfamiliar names surfaced through the crackling static. There was definitely someone, or something, on the other end of the line. He recited them. She was searching for them. Its voice was oppressive and cold, as if dragging a rusty hospital bed through a morgue.

t_ch: 他是谁？
t_en: Who is he?

t_ch: 她是谁？
t_en: Who is she?

t_ch: 它是谁？
t_en: Who is it?

t_ch: 他们是谁？
t_en: Who are they?

t_ch: 你毫无头绪，又或者头绪过于繁杂。名册还在继续延伸。十、百、千、万。他在找他们，但为什么？若她还在世，他们曾有何种交集？如果它早已不在人间，那么……
t_en: You're completely clueless, or maybe drowning in too many clues. The list keeps growing. Ten, a hundred, a thousand, ten thousand. He's searching for them, but why? If she's still alive, what connection do they share? And if it's already gone, then...

t_ch: 了无生气的白噪音正渐渐在狭小的电话亭里滋生一种异样的、黑暗的阴谋。冷汗。凉意。天色暗了下来。幻月还在天上吗？它在看着吗？在看哪里？他们？他？她？它？你？我？
t_en: The lifeless white noise is gradually breeding a strange, dark conspiracy in the cramped phone booth. Cold sweat. A chill. The sky darkens. Is the Phantasmoon still up in the sky? Is it watching? Watching where? Them? Him? Her? It? You? Me?

t_ch: 再一次。尖锐。低沉。凄厉。密集。嘶哑。短促。狰狞。沉闷。耳语。近了。越来越近。他就在附近。她在那里。它在找你。他找到了你。她在你身边。它就要开口了——
t_en: Again. Sharp. Deep. Shrill. Dense. Hoarse. Abrupt. Grotesque. Muffled. Whispers. Closer. Getting closer. He's nearby. She's there. It's looking for you. He found you. She's by your side. It's about to speak...

t_ch: 怎么了，我亲爱的乘客朋友！请问，还有什么事情需要我的帮助吗？
t_en: What can I do for you, my dear passenger? Is there anything else you need help with?

t_ch: 我记得你，一条猫先生！
t_en: I remember you! You're Mr. Wonton!

t_ch: 我不是一条猫，更不是一猫条，而是…一位猫条。希望您能记得。
t_en: I'm not a snack, and I'm certainly not a "wonton." I am... Meowton. I'd appreciate it if you could remember that.

t_ch: 我记得你，车站的站长！
t_en: I remember you! You're the station manager!

t_ch: 哈哈，您记得没错，很高兴为您服务。
t_en: Haha, that's right. It is my pleasure to be of service.

t_ch: 我记得你…虽然我忘了！
t_en: I remember you... kind of!

t_ch: 那您其实就是不记得了，我知道您平时忙，很多人想不起来情有可原。
t_en: In other words, you've forgotten. No worries at all. I imagine you meet so many people every day.

t_ch: 请允许我再次自我介绍一番，我是泊地站的站长——猫条。负责维系泊地站每日乘客往来交通。
t_en: Please allow me to introduce myself again. I'm the station manager of Perdido Station, Meowton. I'm in charge of the daily passenger traffic here.

t_ch: 如果遇到有延误的情况，欢迎您随时来找我。
t_en: If you run into any delays, feel free to come find me anytime.

t_ch: 对了，我还记得你们初来泊地站的情况，那些热情的粉丝可是把咱这围了个水泄不通，您还得是走咱们的「员工通道」才顺利离开。
t_en: Oh, and I remember your arrival at Perdido Station. All those enthusiastic fans had this place completely swarmed. You had to take the "staff only" route just to get out.

t_ch: 希望您现在能比之前轻松不少。
t_en: I hope things are much easier for you now.

t_ch: 上次还是多谢你了！
t_en: Thanks again for your help last time!

t_ch: 哪里哪里，你们是二相乐园重要的客人，这是我作为站长应该做的，哈哈哈！
t_en: Don't mention it! For our honored guests in Planarcadia, it's all part of the service. Hahaha!

t_ch: 顺带一提！我是二维市第一铁道迷，你们所驾驶的星穹列车，是我心中排名历史前五的经典款列车——梦中情车停靠在月台上，其实我蛮幸运的！
t_en: By the way! I'm the biggest train fan in Duomension City. The Astral Express you all ride is one of my top five favorite classic models. I'm so lucky to have my dream train parked at my platform!

t_ch: 不多说了，如果您没有别的事情，我就准备接着工作了！
t_en: Anyway, if there's nothing else, I should get back to work!

t_ch: 欢迎来到泊地站，亲爱的乘客{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Dear {NICKNAME}, welcome to Perdido Station.

t_ch: 我想问你…
t_en: I'm wondering...

t_ch: 当然，您想问些什么呢？
t_en: Of course. What would you like to know?

t_ch: 你说自己是铁道迷？
t_en: You said you're a train geek?

t_ch: 纠正一下，我是二维市第一铁道迷。
t_en: Correction: I'm the number one train fan in Duomension City.

t_ch: 在这里，没人可以说他比猫条更懂铁道。
t_en: No one here knows more about trains than Meowton.

t_ch: 我拍摄过的列车可以办摄影展，我能闭着眼画出整个二相乐园的铁路线路，我还上传了铁路{RUBY_B#第一视角乘坐}POV{RUBY_E#}视频，获得很多点赞。
t_en: I've taken enough pictures of trains to fill a photo exhibit. I can draw the entire Planarcadia railway map with my eyes closed. And I've uploaded train {RUBY_B#Point-of-View}POV{RUBY_E#} videos that got tons of likes.

t_ch: 更绝的是什么，你知道吗？我能完整模拟列车驾驶的所有声音，包括行走音、汽笛声，以及各种报站的声音——
t_en: And you know what the best part is? I can perfectly imitate all the sounds of a train, including the chugging, the whistle, and all the different announcements—

t_ch: 猫条眼神锐利，似乎在暗示你让它当众表演一番。
t_en: Meowton's eyes sharpen, seeming to hint that you should ask for a demonstration.

t_ch: 你能演示下「满嘴跑火车」吗？
t_en: Can you show me your "train impression"?

t_ch: 你注意到，一名真正的铁道迷突然面色凝重，仿佛被列车神附身。
t_en: You notice the train fan's face become serious, as if the spirit of the locomotive itself has taken hold.

t_ch: 「…请不要抢着上车。」
t_en: "Please do not rush to board the train."

t_ch: *突然蒸汽迸发的声*，*明快的电子警告声*嘟嘟嘟，*列车的长笛声渐入*呜嗡——
t_en: *Sudden burst of steam* *sharp electronic warning* Beep-beep-beep *the long whistle of the train fades in* Whoooo—

t_ch: *有节奏的轨道运行声*库库——库库——*激烈的风声*……
t_en: *Rhythmic sound of the moving train* Ch—Chug... ch—chug... *wind roar*...

t_ch: *亲切的女性声音*「感谢您乘坐二维市地铁列车疾锋龙号，本次列车开往海原市，下一站是泊地站。」
t_en: *Pleasant female voice* "Thank you for riding the Bullet Wyrm express of the Duomension City Subway. This train is bound for Seafeld City. Next stop... Perdido Station."

t_ch: *激烈的风声*，*列车减速时巨大的摩擦声*……
t_en: *Wind roar* *loud screech of the train slowing down*...

t_ch: *亲切的女性声音*「请注意脚下，出口在左侧车门。」
t_en: *Pleasant female voice* "Please mind the gap. The exit is on your left."

t_ch: *含糊不清、语速飞快的列车长声音*「请注意脚下，列车就要进站了」。
t_en: *Muffled, rapid-fire conductor's voice* "Please mind the gap, the train is now approaching the platform."

t_ch: *有节奏的轨道运行声变慢*库、库——库、库——*像是卸掉气压的巨大摩擦声*，*明快的电子警告声*嘟嘟嘟……
t_en: *Rhythmic track sounds slows down* Chug... chug... *loud hissing sound like releasing air brakes* *sharp electronic warning* Beep-beep-beep...

t_ch: *稳重的、带有回响的男性声音*「泊地站，泊地站到了。」
t_en: *Steady, echoing male voice* "We are arriving at... Perdido Station."

t_ch: 一人、一桌、一椅、一扇、一抚尺…啊不对，应是一人、一喇叭而已。
t_en: And then there's one person, one table, one chair, one fan, one gavel... Wait, that's not right. It's just one person and one megaphone.

t_ch: 两股战战，几欲先走！
t_en: My knees are knocking... I think I'd rather be leaving now!

t_ch: 过奖，过奖了！
t_en: Oh, you're flattering me!

t_ch: 你刚才说啥，我戴着耳机…
t_en: What did you just say? I had my headphones in...

t_ch: 你看见如此和善的脸上露出了一丝很容易察觉的愤怒。
t_en: You see a flash of obvious anger on their otherwise-friendly face.

t_ch: 不用演示了，我感受到了。
t_en: Okay, okay, I get it. You don't have to show me.

t_ch: 你注意到，一名真正的铁道迷满脸遗憾，它的表演欲望被压制住了。
t_en: You notice the train fan looks disappointed, their burning need to perform now extinguished.

t_ch: 现在，你相信我是二维市第一铁道迷了吧。
t_en: Now, do you believe that I'm the best train fan in all of Duomension City?

t_ch: 能介绍下泊地站吗？
t_en: Could you tell me more about Perdido Station?

t_ch: 这是我工作的地方，也是我热爱的地方。
t_en: This is where I work, and I love it.

t_ch: 曾经这里被称为「幽灵车站」，原因无外乎官方线路图上没有这个站，或者是站台上空无一人，或者手机没信号……
t_en: This place was once called the "Ghost Station" for no other reason than the fact that it wasn't on the official route map. Or if there was no one on the platform, or no signal on your phone...

t_ch: 那时人们还将「去泊地站」叫作「前往异世界」。
t_en: At that time, people even called Perdido Station the "Gate to Another World."

t_ch: 是勤劳的政府，是热心的乘客，与我们携手改变了这些负面印象！如今「泊地站」一片生机勃勃、秩序井然的样子，离不开大家的帮助。
t_en: It was the hardworking government and the passionate passengers who helped turn things around! The vibrant, orderly Perdido Station you see today wouldn't exist without everyone's efforts.

t_ch: 最近有传言说，有不少乘客走不出8号出口。真是好笑…我们根本就没有8号出口！希望流言止于智者。
t_en: Lately, there's been a rumor going around that some passengers can't find their way out of Exit 8. Ridiculous... We don't even have an Exit 8! Let's hope the rumor stops with those who know better.

t_ch: 你似乎很喜欢星穹列车？
t_en: You seem to really like the Astral Express, huh?

t_ch: 当然，如果要我给历史所有的铁道列车排名的话，星穹列车是能够排进前五的，毫无疑问是我的梦中情车。
t_en: Of course! If I had to rank all the trains in history, the Astral Express would definitely make it into my top five. It's my dream train.

t_ch: 星际旅行的列车车头，采用了经典复古的造型——我认为仅凭这一点来看，「开拓」的审美就是非常在线的。
t_en: The front of this interstellar beauty carries a classic retro design. I think that alone proves the Trailblaze's aesthetic is absolutely on point.

t_ch: 只是前五而已吗？
t_en: Only top five?

t_ch: 我的评价已经非常高了。对不起，关于这点我绝不动摇。
t_en: That's already very high praise. Sorry, I won't back down on that.

t_ch: 那你可得羡慕我呀？
t_en: Then you must envy me, huh?

t_ch: 我听说您在列车上还有个私人房间！你是世界上最幸福的人！
t_en: I heard you've even got a private room on the Express! You're the luckiest person alive!

t_ch: 谢谢，我没什么想问的了。
t_en: Thanks, I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 没问题，和您聊天很愉快，有需要帮助的地方请开口。
t_en: No problem. It's been great chatting with you. If you need anything, just let me know.

t_ch: 谢谢，我没什么想问的了。
t_en: Thanks, I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 没问题，和您聊天很愉快，有需要帮助的地方请开口。
t_en: No problem. It's been great chatting with you. If you need anything, just let me know.

t_ch: 欢迎乘坐疾锋龙列车「超级快」号，星穹列车的客人们。请坐稳扶好，不要乱跑！先下后上，有序乘车！
t_en: Welcome aboard Bullet Wyrm "The Superfast"! Passengers of the Astral Express, please hold tight and don't run around! Disembark first, board later! Let's keep things orderly!

t_ch: 想问你点事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 为乘客提供服务是我的荣幸！但请快一些，不要耽误疾锋龙列车的正常运行。
t_en: It's an honor to serve you all! But please, ask quickly. We can't hold up the Bullet Wyrm Express's schedule.

t_ch: 疾锋龙一族都是列车吗？
t_en: So, is every Bullet Wyrm an express car?

t_ch: 疾锋龙一族是列车形的幻造种，自然也大都以「列车」为天职。但并非所有疾锋龙都屈从于天职。
t_en: The Bullet Wyrms are train-shaped imagenae, so yes, most of us take "express trains" as our vocation. But not every Bullet Wyrm submits to fate.

t_ch: 最近刚有一位名叫「托马仕」的疾锋龙，成为了二相乐园治安局第一位疾锋龙治安官。
t_en: Recently, a Bullet Wyrm named Thomas became the first Bullet Wyrm security officer in the Planarcadia Security Bureau.

t_ch: 只不过托马仕的体型过于巨大，无法参与日常执勤，梦想中的火车追凶戏码也从没发生…唉，这家伙很值得敬佩，但它的追梦之路道阻且长啊！
t_en: It's just that Thomas is a bit too... well, oversized for regular duty, and his dream train-chase scenes never happened... *sigh* He's truly admirable, but his path to his dream will be a long one!

t_ch: 至于我嘛…哇哈哈哈，在轨道上奔驰就是我的天性，我并不打算违背它！
t_en: As for me, wahahaha! Racing along the tracks is in my nature. I have no intention of going against it!

t_ch: 你和星穹列车哪个比较快？
t_en: Who's faster, you or the Astral Express?

t_ch: 哼，听你的口气，似乎很轻视我的速度嘛～
t_en: Hmph, sounds like you're underestimating me!

t_ch: 你们星穹列车的跃迁能力我是比不了，但若是只比拼在轨道上奔跑的速度，我还真不一定会输！
t_en: Sure, I can't match the Astral Express's warp speed, but if it's just a race along the rails, I might just hold my own!

t_ch: 毕竟，虽然星穹列车是星神造物，但我们幻造种也是星神造物啊！乐子神和阿基维利谁跑得比较快？我觉得还得是我们的乐子神。
t_en: After all, while the Astral Express is an Aeonspawn, so are we imagenae! Between The Laughter and Akivili, who's faster? I'd bet on The Laughter.

t_ch: 把列车炸成两截之后，也没见祂被阿基维利撵上啊。
t_en: It's not like Akivili ever managed to catch THEM, even after THEY blew the Express clean in half.

t_ch: 不过口说无凭，等何时有时间，倒是可以找机会比试一番。哈哈哈哈！
t_en: But talk is cheap. When there's time, we should definitely have a proper race! Hahahaha!

t_ch: 你们下班后住哪里啊？
t_en: Where do you go after work?

t_ch: 下班？哼，只有上班不快乐的人才想着下班！
t_en: After work? Hmph, only people who don't enjoy their jobs think about getting off work!

t_ch: 对我们疾锋龙而言，工作是充斥着幸福与快乐的。每时每刻，我们都被疾驰的喜悦充盈着，一点都不想下班！
t_en: For us, our work is filled with joy. It's our greatest happiness. Every moment, we're full of the exhilaration of racing at full speed. Why would we ever want to stop!?

t_ch: 更何况，那些在我们体内的乘客，也在持续用自己的愿力滋养着我们。
t_en: Besides, the passengers within us nourish us with their Wishpower.

t_ch: 当我们不早不晚，准时准点地将乘客送到目的地时，他们的幸福与快乐也会流向我们，让我们充满力量！
t_en: When we deliver them right on time, not early, not late, their joy and happiness flow into us, fueling our strength!

t_ch: 哼，「下班」…请别再让我听到这么没出息的词语了！
t_en: Hmph, "off work"? Don't let me hear such a phrase of losers again!

t_ch: 你先忙吧，我走了。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work. I must be going now.

t_ch: 再见！欢迎你随时搭乘疾锋龙列车「超级快」。
t_en: Goodbye! You're always welcome aboard Bullet Wyrm "The Superfast"!

t_ch: 你先忙吧，我走了。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work. I must be going now.

t_ch: 再见！欢迎你随时搭乘疾锋龙列车「超级快」。
t_en: Goodbye! You're always welcome aboard Bullet Wyrm "The Superfast"!

t_ch: 你好，亲爱的无名客！我是「小鳄鱼侦探俱乐部」的理事长。鄙俱乐部长期提供各种侦探服务，正适合你这种每天都在惹麻烦的无名客！
t_en: Hello there, my dear Nameless! I'm the director of Detective Croc Club. My club has long provided a variety of detective services, which are perfect for a Nameless like you who's always getting into trouble!

t_ch: 我那叫「卷入麻烦」好吗？
t_en: It's called "getting caught up in trouble," okay?

t_ch: 请不必在意这些细节。毕竟无论主动还是被动，「麻烦」的本质都不会变。
t_en: Potato, po-tah-to. Call it what you want. The point is, you've got troubles, and I've got solutions.

t_ch: 你能帮我解决什么麻烦？
t_en: So what can you do for me?

t_ch: 寻人、讨债、调查真相…其他侦探能做到的工作，我同样能做到。并且，我会做得比他们更加优秀～
t_en: Finding people, collecting debts, investigating the truth... Any case the other guys can take, I can crack. And I'm better than them, guaranteed~

t_ch: 你听说过「犯罪侧写」吗？从刚才见面起，我就一直在打量你，适当打理的灰色头发，一丝不苟又带有些许随意的穿着，如果大侦探没有推理错的话——
t_en: Ever heard of criminal profiling? I've been sizing you up ever since we met. Neatly styled gray hair, meticulous yet casually elegant attire. If this great detective's deduction is correct...

t_ch: 你也是「银河战力党」的玩家吧？我现在手头有很多「银河战力党」的相关商品亟需出手，又不方便找正规门店……
t_en: You're a Cosmicon Collective player, aren't you? I happen to have a lot of Cosmicon Collective merch I need to get rid of, and it's not exactly convenient to go through the proper channels...

t_ch: 你这卖的是赃物啊？
t_en: Are you selling stolen property?

t_ch: 那怎么可能呢？我这都是堂堂正正从附近小孩手里赢来的！是战利品！
t_en: Of course not! I won all this fair and square from the kids around here! They're my trophies!

t_ch: 这跟你的侧写有什么关系？
t_en: What's that got to do with your profiling?

t_ch: 诶，俗话说的好——推理模块基础，那商务模块就不能基础。你就让让我呗，卖卡嘛，不寒碜。
t_en: You know, they always say if you've got the basics down for detective work, you can't slack off on the business side either. So cut me some slack, yeah? I'm just selling cards. Nothing embarrassing about that.

t_ch: 总之，请记住这件事，亲爱的无名客——在二相乐园的几乎每座城市，你都能看到「小鳄鱼侦探俱乐部」的海报。只要找到那张海报，你就能找到我～
t_en: Anyway, my dear Nameless, just remember this: You can find posters for Detective Croc Club in almost every city in Planarcadia. Find the poster, and you'll find me~

t_ch: 无论你需要侦探服务，还是想要购买物美价廉的「银河战力党」相关商品，都可以来找我鳄鱼侦探。「小鳄鱼侦探俱乐部」一定会给到你最优质的服务。
t_en: Whether you need a detective or want to buy some quality, affordable Cosmicon Collective merch, you can always come to me. Detective Croc Club guarantees the best service you can get.

t_ch: 欢迎光临，亲爱的无名客！「小鳄鱼侦探俱乐部」竭诚为您服务！今天有侦探委托？还是要买点「银河战力党」的相关商品？
t_en: Welcome, my dear Nameless! Detective Croc Club is at your service! Do you have a case for me today? Or are you looking to buy some Cosmicon Collective merch?

t_ch: 看看你的「银河战力党」商店。
t_en: I'd like to see your Cosmicon Collective stock.

t_ch: 多谢惠顾，亲爱的无名客～
t_en: Thanks for your business, my dear Nameless~

t_ch: 我有点好奇…
t_en: There's something I'm curious about...

t_ch: 你问吧。毕竟「满足好奇心」也算是侦探的使命。
t_en: Ask away. Indulging a little curiosity is part of the job for a detective.

t_ch: 你的俱乐部在哪里？
t_en: Where's your club?

t_ch: 你面前的这张海报，就是我的俱乐部。二维市的我将自己二维化，居住在这张二维的海报里。这样一来，就有了两个好处——
t_en: You're looking at it. This poster is my club. In Duomension City, I made myself 2D to live inside this poster, which brings along two great benefits.

t_ch: 第一，房租便宜，生活成本低。二维化的幻造种既不占地方，也不怎么消耗愿力。
t_en: First, the rent is cheap, so my living expenses are low. A 2D imagenae doesn't take up any space, and I barely use any Wishpower.

t_ch: 第二，我在二相乐园各处都张贴了自己的海报。这样无论你去哪座城市的哪条街道，只要找到这张海报，就能找到我鳄鱼侦探！每时每刻，都能为我的顾客服务～
t_en: Second, I've put my posters up all over Planarcadia. That means no matter what city or street you're on, if you find this poster, you've found Detective Croc! I can serve my customers anytime, anywhere~

t_ch: 但是也有一些坏处啦。比如这样一来，我就没办法亲赴现场调查了。
t_en: There are a few downsides, though. For one, I can't go to a scene to investigate in person.

t_ch: 其实我尝试过，比如把海报做成风筝，飞翔到城市上空吗，用俯瞰的方式进行流动的思考，我称之为「俯瞰流考」。
t_en: I actually tried it once. I turned my poster into a kite so I could fly over the city, letting my thoughts flow while I looked down from above. That's what I call my "Elevated Thinking."

t_ch: 但结果嘛…「放飞自我」的行动让我差点没把出生时的愿力都吐出来。
t_en: But as it turns out... that "letting loose" action nearly made me puke up every ounce of Wishpower I had.

t_ch: 所以从此以后，我决定还是安心做个「安乐椅侦探」。实在需要去现场，就委托别人替我我去吧。
t_en: So after that, I decided I was better off as an "armchair detective." If I really need someone on site, I'll just hire them to go for me.

t_ch: 这城市的侦探好多啊！
t_en: There are a ton of detectives in this city!

t_ch: 是啊，二维市的「侦探」可太多了。这帮侦探甚至还组建了一个叫「二维市名侦探摸鱼潜水群」的团体，避免互相抢生意。
t_en: Yep, Duomension City is crawling with "detectives." They even formed a group called the "Duomension City Detective Slackers & Lurkers" to keep from stepping on each other's toes.

t_ch: 有些侦探实在太有名，甚至还有自己的粉丝团体呢！不过他们大多都是抓出轨，抓小偷小窃上不了台面的案件。
t_en: Some of them are so famous they've even got their own fan clubs! Though most of them just handle small-time stuff like catching adulterers and petty thieves, nothing exactly glamorous.

t_ch: 哼，依我看，这二维市的侦探千千万，能配得上「名侦探」称号的只有三位——绘世学院的「青蛙白板」、鸽川区的「不死途」以及市中心的「鳄鱼侦探」。
t_en: Hmph, if you ask me, out of all the detectives in Duomension City, only three deserve to be called a "Great Detective": Froggy Whiteboard from Graphia Academy, Ashveil from Dovebrook District, and Detective Croc from downtown.

t_ch: 可惜呀、可惜，青蛙白板早已退隐江湖，不死途又好逸恶劳，如今能够独挑大梁的，只剩下我鳄鱼侦探而已。
t_en: What a shame... Froggy Whiteboard retired a long time ago, and Ashveil is just plain lazy. The only one left to carry the mantle is me, Detective Croc.

t_ch: 你为啥在做「战力党」的生意？
t_en: Why are you dealing in Cosmicon Collective stuff?

t_ch: 做侦探实在太穷了…我真是因为热爱，才一直在做侦探业。要不然早就转行了！
t_en: Being a detective pays terribly... I'm only still doing it because I love it. I would've switched careers ages ago otherwise!

t_ch: 我是「影鳄」一族的幻造种。我们这个种族，是公司来航以后出现的新种族，诞生于二相乐园人对公司复杂的感情，
t_en: I'm a shadowcroc imagenae. We're a new race that appeared after the IPC came here, born from the complicated feelings the people of Planarcadia have for the IPC.

t_ch: 你应该没怎么见过影鳄吧？因为我大部分同族都选择了金融、证券或投资之类的工作，和我们生活在不同的世界。
t_en: You probably haven't seen many shadowcrocs, have you? Most of my kin went into finance, securities, or investment. They live in a totally different world from us.

t_ch: 不过，没有什么种族生下来就必须做什么，我们可以是任何人…所以，为了追梦，我成为了一名侦探。
t_en: But no race is born having to be something. We can be anyone we want... So to chase my dream, I became a detective.

t_ch: …话虽这么说，但穷是真穷，饿也是真饿。所以最终我还是为生计做出了妥协，参与起了「银河战力党」相关的投机倒把……
t_en: Still, a man's gotta eat, and ideals don't pay the bills. Eventually, I had to get my hands dirty and get into flipping Cosmicon Collective merch...

t_ch: 往好处想，至少喜欢「银河战力党」的朋友找到一位可靠的卡牌商人——说话好听，长得可爱，价格实惠，还会点小推理，不是吗？
t_en: If you look on the bright side, at least fans of the Cosmicon Collective have found themselves a reliable card merchant. Sweet-talker, cute as a button, fair prices, and a knack for deduction to boot. What's not to like?

t_ch: 暂时没别的事了。
t_en: I have no other business for the time being.

t_ch: 亲爱的无名客，你要是听完我的心路历程，多少也有点触动的话…就请你多多照顾我的生意吧，嘿嘿。
t_en: My dear Nameless, if my heartfelt story moved you at all... then please, be a good customer, hehe.

t_ch: 暂时没别的事了。
t_en: I have no other business for the time being.

t_ch: 亲爱的无名客，你要是听完我的心路历程，多少也有点触动的话…就请你多多照顾我的生意吧，嘿嘿。
t_en: My dear Nameless, if my heartfelt story moved you at all... then please, be a good customer, hehe.

t_ch: 哎呀，小同学…看你一直*盯*着我，莫非……
t_en: Oh my, little one... You've been *staring* at me this whole time. Could it be...

t_ch: 你是觉得…<color=#eb4d3dff>我很漂亮吗？</color>
t_en: You think... <color=#eb4d3dff>I'm pretty?</color>

t_ch: 漂亮。
t_en: Pretty.

t_ch: 在听到你的赞美后，眼前的妖怪怔住了。你仿佛看到它那仅存于设计草稿中因种种现实原因（很可能是资本）未能呈现给观众的眉毛扬了起来，恣意潇洒。
t_en: The strangeling before you freezes at your compliment. You can almost see its eyebrows, which existed only in the concept sketches and were never shown to the public due to practical reasons (capitalism, perhaps), lift with carefree grace.

t_ch: 不漂亮。
t_en: Not at all.

t_ch: 在听到你的批评后，眼前的妖怪怔住了，似乎早在你开口前就已经接受了事实。它简约的瞳仁此刻仿佛就是*沉默*本身：漆黑、深邃，且都有六个。
t_en: The strangeling before you freezes at your criticism, as though it had already accepted the truth before you even spoke. Its simple eyes now seem to embody *silence* itself: dark, deep, and all six of them.

t_ch: 很诡异！
t_en: So weird!

t_ch: 在听到「诡异」二字后，眼前的妖怪怔住了。它简约的瞳仁霎时间变得极具信息量——感性地说就是*浑浊*——随后扩张。像墨滴污染清水，直径前后变化约为<unbreak>0.75</unbreak>毫米。
t_en: At the mention of "weird," the strangeling freezes again. Its simple eyes suddenly brim with meaning: emotionally speaking, they turn *murky*, then dilate, like ink spreading through clear water, expanding by about <unbreak>0.75</unbreak> millimeters in diameter.

t_ch: 你知道，那是愤怒的信号。
t_en: You know that's a sign of anger.

t_ch: …诡异？哦，我宁可诡异，也不愿意做二相乐园满地都是的可怜幻造种。概念无趣，相貌平平、平庸、庸俗、俗媚、媚俗、俗媚、媚……
t_en: ...Weird? Oh, I'd choose "weird" any day over being one of those pitiful imagenae cluttering up Planarcadia. Boring concepts, plain faces, mediocre, tacky, vulgar, kitschy...

t_ch: 在一阵激烈的词语接龙后，它肉眼不可见地不再激动了。
t_en: After a sharp word chain, it becomes almost imperceptible that the creature stops being agitated.

t_ch: …咳，失礼了。很高兴你也那么爱艺术并且有自己的见解。
t_en: ...Ahem, sorry. I'm glad to see you love art so much and have your own unique perspective.

t_ch: 正式做个自我介绍吧：我是绘世学院第<unbreak>24</unbreak>届先锋艺术探索周暨二维市吉祥物大赛参展作品，视肉涂鸦。
t_en: Let me introduce myself properly: I'm Visaffiti, a piece from the <unbreak>24</unbreak>th Pioneer Art Exploration Week and the Duomension City Mascot Competition Exhibition at Graphia Academy.

t_ch: 如你所见，我是一件艺术品。这里是我的常设展览位，如果你喜欢小众先锋艺术，陶冶情操，欢迎随时来*欣赏*我。
t_en: As you can see, I'm a work of art. This is my permanent exhibition. If you like avant-garde art to cultivate your character. Feel free to come and *admire* me at any time.

t_ch: 愿我能成为你的灵感缪斯，而不是那个「诡异的涂鸦」。
t_en: I hope I can be your muse of inspiration, not that "weird graffiti."

t_ch: 又见面了，我就那么让你过*目*不忘吗？
t_en: We meet again. Do I really that impressive in your *eyes*?

t_ch: …不好笑吗？好吧。
t_en: ...Is it not funny? Fine.

t_ch: 我有点好奇…
t_en: There's something I'm curious about...

t_ch: 那个什么大赛…是什么？
t_en: What's that... Competition?

t_ch: 是绘世学院第<unbreak>24</unbreak>届先锋艺术探索周暨第<unbreak>2025</unbreak>届二维市吉祥物大赛。*顾*名思义，它本该是一场先锋艺术家专属的阳春白雪，却在公司运作下成了人工智能的狂欢。
t_en: It's the 24th Pioneer Art Exploration Week and the 2025 Duomension City Mascot Competition Exhibition at Graphia Academy. As you can see, it was supposed to be an avant-garde event for elite artists, yet intervened by the IPC, it became a celebration for artificial intelligence.

t_ch: 哎！资本。艺术已死。「欢愉」已死。
t_en: Ah! Capitalism. Art is dead. "Elation" is dead.

t_ch: 谁说不是呢！我同情你。
t_en: Who can argue with that! I feel for you.

t_ch: 说真的，我一直在想：「纯美」陨落，公司也肯定脱不了干系。
t_en: Honestly, I've often thought: the fall of "Beauty," the IPC is surely behind it.

t_ch: 有考虑过从自己身上找原因吗？
t_en: You ever thought you might be the reason?

t_ch: 噢，当然。但如果这么想，一切都说不通！
t_en: Oh, of course. But if I think that way, nothing makes sense!

t_ch: 像我这么弱的幻造种，怎么有能力阻碍艺术的发展呢？再说了，我可是受害者，一*目*了然。
t_en: How could a weak imagenae like me ever stand in the way of art? Besides, I'm the victim here. Can't you *see* it?

t_ch: 你还是没解释清楚这个…大赛。
t_en: You still haven't explained this... Competition clearly.

t_ch: 噢，因为这不重要。所有比赛本质上都是黑幕。
t_en: Oh, because it's not important. All competitions are essentially rigged.

t_ch: 这么想能让很多事情变简单很多。我为艺术而生，逻辑推理不是我的强项。
t_en: Thinking this way makes things much simpler. I was born for art. Logical reasoning is not my strength.

t_ch: 你为什么长这样？
t_en: Why do you look like this?

t_ch: 很多人都问过这个问题。毕竟，我这副模样在幻造种里算不上…呃，健康。
t_en: A lot of people have asked me that. After all, I'm not exactly considered... well, healthy by imagenae standards.

t_ch: 许多幻造种的生命走到尽头时，都会变成一坨像素，或者一滩颜料。但我恰恰相反，我天生就长这样——因为我主题如此。
t_en: When many imagenae near the end of their lives, they turn into pixels or blobs of paint. But I'm different. I've always looked like this, and that's my theme.

t_ch: 「《视肉涂鸦》表现了一种二相乐园、或者说画中世界视角下介于现实与梦境、新生与死亡之间的超现实境界，这无疑是一种潜意识世界的真诚反应……」
t_en: "Visaffiti represents a surreal space between reality and dreams, between life and death, viewed through the lens of Planarcadia or the World in Canvas. It's a true reflection of the subconscious..."

t_ch: 很可惜，我的创造者没有把这些话写在展签上——那家伙只说参考了匹诺康尼的爱德华医生，然后给我套了个和丰饶孽物一样的名字。
t_en: Sadly, my creator didn't write this on the exhibition sign. My creator was just inspired by Dr. Edward from Penacony, and gave me a name like an Abominations of Abundance.

t_ch: 「百百」是谁？
t_en: Who is "Unodred"?

t_ch: 二维市曾经风靡一时的形象大使，和我长得特别像：区别只是比我多了<unbreak>94</unbreak>只眼睛、一张嘴、一双手和一双脚。
t_en: Visaffiti was the once-popular mascot of Duomension City. It looks a lot like me, except it has <unbreak>94</unbreak> extra eyes, a mouth, hands, and feet.

t_ch: 说真的，我们要是放在一起，你肯定分不出谁是谁——但它就是能拿下当年的大赛冠军。
t_en: Honestly, if we were placed side by side, you wouldn't be able to tell us apart, yet it won that year's Competition.

t_ch: 为什么？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 抛开那些肮脏的交易不谈——它不懂得克制的美。
t_en: Putting those dirty deals aside, it doesn't understand the beauty of restraint.

t_ch: 听起来…区别还挺大。
t_en: Sounds like... there's quite a difference.

t_ch: 提示一下：区分我们的关键词是「克制」。
t_en: A hint: the key to distinguishing us is "restraint."

t_ch: 感觉有点眼熟。
t_en: Looks familiar.

t_ch: 这或许就是是既*视*感吧。毕竟它和我实在是太像了，却不懂克制的美。
t_en: Maybe it's just *visual* recognition. After all, it looks so much like me, but it doesn't understand the beauty of restraint.

t_ch: 眼睛越多越好，颜色越亮越美，还有一大堆莫名其妙的包装：什么「百百」和一百只眼睛象征了二相乐园源远流长的历史和文化，银河无数思想的碰撞、交融……
t_en: The more eyes, the better; the brighter the colors, the more charming. And then there's all this nonsense packaging: something like "Unodred's" hundred eyes symbolize Planarcadia's long history and culture, the endless clash and fusion of thoughts across the galaxy...

t_ch: 不仅是这些，他甚至还有一套完整的人设！大赛评委听完都感动哭了。
t_en: And it even has a full character design! The judges were moved to tears hearing this.

t_ch: 后来，我的创作者也哭了——倒不是因为共情或者落选，只是语文成绩太差被劝退了。
t_en: Later, my creator cried too, not because of empathy or losing, but because his language grades were so bad he was kicked out.

t_ch: 那一天，身为艺术家，他成长了许多。
t_en: That day, as an artist, he grew a lot.

t_ch: 你了解绘世学院吗？
t_en: You know anything about Graphia Academy?

t_ch: 当然了解，我就在这里兼职当监控摄像头和心理疏导专家——顺便一提，主职是艺术品。
t_en: Of course I do. I work part-time here as a surveillance camera and a psychological counselor. My main job, though, is being an artwork.

t_ch: 想听什么八卦，我这里都有：你想听朱蒂同学和尼克同学的爱恨情仇，还是弥尔汉老师在机铠咖啡厅的兼职传闻？
t_en: Want some gossip? I've got plenty. Would you rather hear about the love-hate story between Judy and Nick, or the rumor that Professor Mirhan's working part-time at Mechatron Coffee?

t_ch: 泄露别人隐私不好吧。
t_en: Privacy? It's not cool.

t_ch: 当然，所以打死我也不会和你说的。
t_en: Of course, which is why I'd never tell you.

t_ch: 哼哼，已经有好几个学生被我折磨得抓心挠肝，一连几天没睡好了。
t_en: Heh, a few students are already losing sleep because of me, tossing and turning for days.

t_ch: 我想了解一点…更严肃的。
t_en: I'm hoping to learn something... a bit more serious.

t_ch: 校史？那你找错人了。这得找幻造课教室的<color=#dbc291ff>啵啵娃</color>老师。
t_en: The school's history? Then you've come to the wrong person. You should ask <color=#dbc291ff>Ms. Dollie</color> from the Imagenesis class.

t_ch: 如果是更限制级的…去找档案室的<color=#dbc291ff>青蛙白板</color>吧。我经常被它找去当*目击*证人，见过这所学校太多黑暗面……
t_en: If you're after something more... restricted, go see Froggy Whiteboard in the archive room. I've been called in as a witness by that one plenty of times, seen more of this school's dark side...

t_ch: 但根据劳务合同保密协议第三条<unbreak>24</unbreak>款，我不能透露任何作案方法细节，以免造成剧透。
t_en: But according to Article 3, Clause <unbreak>24</unbreak> of my labor confidentiality agreement, I can't reveal any details of those cases. Spoilers, you know.

t_ch: 今天的艺术欣赏就到这里了。
t_en: That's all for today's art appreciation.

t_ch: 已经充满灵感了吗？真可惜，我还没*看够*你呢。
t_en: Feeling inspired already? What a pity, I still haven't *seen* enough of you.

t_ch: …不好笑吗？好吧。
t_en: ...Is it not funny? Fine.

t_ch: 今天的艺术欣赏就到这里了。
t_en: That's all for today's art appreciation.

t_ch: 已经充满灵感了吗？真可惜，我还没*看够*你呢。
t_en: Feeling inspired already? What a pity, I still haven't *seen* enough of you.

t_ch: …不好笑吗？好吧。
t_en: ...Is it not funny? Fine.

t_ch: 需要我重复几次呢？这位同学，在学校里要保持安静！
t_en: How many times do I have to say it? This is a school, and you need to be quiet!

t_ch: 啊，原来这玩意会说话吗？
t_en: Whoa, so this thing can talk?
Wha—!?

t_ch: 你怎么可以说是「这玩意」呢？看清楚了，我是藏在画中的妖怪！
t_en: How dare you call me a "thing"? Look closer! I am a strangeling hidden in the painting!

t_ch: 大家都说我是个安静{F#女孩}{M#男孩}。
t_en: But everyone says I'm a quiet {F#girl}{M#boy}.

t_ch: 大家说的话，只是大家说的话而已。
t_en: Those are just empty words from empty minds.

t_ch: 我是绘世学院的教导主任海龙老师，你不认识我，我可记得你——
t_en: I am Ms. Seadrake, the Dean of Graphia Academy. You may not recognize me, but I remember you...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你是在开学这天入学的，我记得没错吧？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you enrolled on the first day of school, am I right?

t_ch: …啊？什么意思？不然呢？
t_en: Uh... yeah? And?

t_ch: 您是在生日这天出生的吗？
t_en: And I suppose you were born on your birthday?

t_ch: 嗐，你以为我跟你闹着玩呢，我只是在提醒你，你应该被提醒的事情。
t_en: What, you think I'm joking with you? I am simply reminding you of that which needs reminding.

t_ch: …我观察到一个现象，现在的学生都不太服从管教，多说一句都不爱听。
t_en: I've noticed a trend: Students today are not receptive to discipline. They don't want to hear a single word more than necessary.

t_ch: 你是不是嫌我啰嗦了？
t_en: Are you saying I'm being long-winded?

t_ch: 其实我也有反省过，我经常说着自己关心绘世学院的学生，但我总是在开会，让同学们感觉到有一些距离感，也是自然的。
t_en: Well, actually, I have reflected on this. I always say that I care about the students here at Graphia Academy, but I'm constantly in meetings. It's only natural that they feel a certain distance from me.

t_ch: …这位同学，我也想多了解一些现在的年轻人，让我们再聊聊吧——
t_en: Hey, I do wish to learn more about the young people of today. Let us continue our conversation...

t_ch: 同学！有什么需要我帮助的吗？你可以完全不用客气，也就是说，你可以敞开说。
t_en: Hey there! Is there anything you need my help with? There is no need for pleasantries, which is to say, you may speak freely.

t_ch: 让我们简单聊聊吧…
t_en: Let's have a talk...

t_ch: 好呀，让我们多聊聊。
t_en: Of course, let's talk some more.

t_ch: 教导主任一般做什么呢？
t_en: What does a dean normally do?

t_ch: 通常来说是确保教学质量，教学计划、督导、研究和改革…另外就是学生的日常管理，学风建设、学生纪律等各方面都要管。
t_en: Typically, it involves ensuring the quality of education: curriculum planning, supervision, research, reform... and then there's the daily student management, which covers everything from academic conduct to student discipline.

t_ch: 说白了，就是开会。
t_en: In short, it's a lot of meetings.

t_ch: 并不是所有的教导主任都像我这样擅长开会的，你明白吗？在一般人看来，开会很枯燥很无聊，而在我看来，开会是一门艺术，非常先锋。
t_en: Honestly, not all teachers are quite as talented at chairing meetings as I am. Does that make sense? Most people find meetings dull and tedious, but to me, they're a form of art. Very avant-garde.

t_ch: 开会需要先锋吗？
t_en: Since when did meetings need to be avant-garde?

t_ch: 艺术是什么意思？
t_en: And what's that supposed to mean, "art"?

t_ch: 你要我解释艺术是什么意思，这个行为本身就不够先锋。
t_en: The fact that you have to ask what that means is, in itself, not very avant-garde.

t_ch: 您说自己很关心年轻人？
t_en: So you wish to learn more about young people?

t_ch: 没错，相较雷虬这样的大妖怪，我还是个年轻人；相较于机铠们，我又是老前辈…我认为，我就像是年轻一代与年长一代之间的桥梁。
t_en: That's right. Compared to a great strangeling like thundercoil, I'm still young. But compared to the mechatrons, I'm an old-timer... I see myself as a bridge between the younger generation and the older one.

t_ch: 这是教学主任应该做的，为此我还学会了年轻人最喜欢的热梗和流行词——
t_en: It's what a dean should do. I've even learned all the popular slang and catchphrases the kids are using...

t_ch: 比如「芭比Q了」、「绝绝子」、「喜大普奔」…怎么样呢？
t_en: Like, uh, "It's cooked," "Totally slay," or "Understood the assignment"... How's that?

t_ch: 感觉有点过时了。
t_en: Those are a bit outdated.

t_ch: 这是什么意思呢？
t_en: What does that mean?

t_ch: 你得上现在的网…
t_en: You need to go online... like, the internet of today...

t_ch: 哈哈，都怪旧新闻的信息太老了。时间过得还真是飞快呀……
t_en: Haha, that's what I get for reading old news. Time really does fly...

t_ch: 说起这个，我还很关心学生们未来的规划，我经常找不同的学生问他们「想象一下<unbreak>13</unbreak>年后的自己到底在做什么？」我希望他们能思考这个问题。
t_en: On that note, I'm also very concerned about my students' future plans. I often ask them, "Imagine what you'll be doing <unbreak>13</unbreak> years from now." I want them to really think about that question.

t_ch: 为什么要思考这个问题？
t_en: But why should they think about that?

t_ch: 你想想，时间会改变很多事，又有很多事不会改变，不是很神奇吗？
t_en: Well, think about it. Time changes so many things, yet some things never change. Isn't that fascinating?

t_ch: 就像从过去开始，我和雷虬的年纪就不断增加，但是惊人的是，我们之间的年龄差却是一点都没有变呢。
t_en: For example, thundercoil and I have been getting older since the beginning of time, but what's amazing is that the age gap between us hasn't changed at all.

t_ch: 我希望年轻人能够畅想未来，思考这其中的变与不变。
t_en: I wish for the young people to envision their futures, and to think about what changes and what stays the same.

t_ch: <unbreak>13</unbreak>年有什么特别的说法？
t_en: Why <unbreak>13</unbreak>, specifically?

t_ch: 没有什么特别的说法，只是我与学生的对话过程中突然福至心灵，在那思维的海洋深处，突然——
t_en: Oh, nothing special. I was just talking to a student when a sudden wave of inspiration hit me from the depths of my mind, and then...

t_ch: 出现了「<unbreak>13</unbreak>」这个数字。
t_en: I just saw the number "<unbreak>13</unbreak>" itself.

t_ch: 真的吗？没有任何实际的意义？
t_en: Seriously? It doesn't actually mean anything?

t_ch: 对的，没有意义构成了它震撼人心的冲击力。和年轻人说话，总是需要一些震撼人心的要素。
t_en: Exactly. The "meaninglessness" is what makes it so punchy. You've got to have that shock factor when you're dealing with the younger crowd.

t_ch: 您有没有觉得自己说话很绕…
t_en: Have you ever noticed that you tend to... talk in circles?

t_ch: 不，我不觉得自己说话很绕。
t_en: No, I don't believe I talk in circles.

t_ch: 我觉得我的说话都是经过思考的，是不假思索的。
t_en: I believe my words are both deliberate and spontaneous.

t_ch: 现在就有点绕…
t_en: This right here is quite confusing...

t_ch: 有点没有听懂…
t_en: Okay, now you've got me in the circles...

t_ch: 很好理解吧？最近我还参加了一场研讨会，被评价为说话一针见血。
t_en: It's easy enough to understand, isn't it? I was just at a seminar recently, and people said I really get straight to the point.

t_ch: 我说「学生翘课这种事，只在课堂上是抓不到人的」，在座的老师纷纷点头。
t_en: I said, "You can't catch students skipping class if you only look in the classroom," and all the teachers there were nodding along.

t_ch: 能不能说点大家不知道的呢？
t_en: How about telling us something we don't already know?

t_ch: …呃，算了算了，你会这么觉得，只是我们之间的脑电波没有对上罢了。
t_en: Ugh... forget it. You only think that because we're not on the same wavelength.

t_ch: 就先聊到这里吧！
t_en: That's it for now!

t_ch: 如果还想和我交流，我随时欢迎，也就是说，你可以随时来找我。
t_en: If you want to talk again, you're always welcome. In other words, you can come find me whenever you want.

t_ch: 就先聊到这里吧！
t_en: That's it for now!

t_ch: 如果还想和我交流，我随时欢迎，也就是说，你可以随时来找我。
t_en: If you want to talk again, you're always welcome. In other words, you can come find me whenever you want.

t_ch: 诶呀，这不是姬子的伙伴吗？火花大会那件事以后，我们还没好好说过几句话呢。
t_en: Oh my, if it isn't Himeko's companion! We haven't had a proper talk since that whole business at the SparxiCon, have we?

t_ch: 说到那件事，我还真是要向你道歉…竟然没能看穿她的阴谋诡计。
t_en: Speaking of which, I really do owe you an apology... I totally didn't see through her schemes and tricks.

t_ch: 都怪火花太狡猾…
t_en: It's all Sparxie's fault. She was too cunning...

t_ch: 为人师表，可不能推卸责任。姬子信任我，才把面具托付给我，这件事说到底是我防范不周啊。
t_en: As her teacher, I cannot shift the blame. Since Himeko entrusted me with the mask, I should've been more careful.

t_ch: 都怪我们来得太晚…
t_en: It's our fault for being too late...

t_ch: 为人师表，可不能推卸责任。姬子信任我，才把面具托付给我，这件事说到底是我做得不妥。
t_en: As her teacher, I cannot shift the blame. Himeko entrusted me with the mask, so this was still my fault.

t_ch: 都怪老师你没看穿！
t_en: It's your fault for not seeing through her!
